deck: ~14 cards — Neutralize+, Survivor, Blade Dance, Leg Sweep, Backflip (innate via Bottled Lightning), Piercing Wail, Dash, Glass Knife, Acrobatics, Strike x3, Defend x3
relics: Ring of the Snake, Lantern (+1E turn 1), Bottled Lightning (Backflip innate), Maw Bank
cause_of_death: Hexaghost at Floor 16. Burns accumulation over 16 turns. Deck had ZERO poison scaling and no damage amplifiers (no Accuracy, no Terror, no Noxious Fumes, no Deadly Poison, no Catalyst). Damage output ~10-18/turn from Glass Knife (degrading), Blade Dance Shivs (4 each), Strikes (6 each), Dash (10). By Turn 12, 3+ Burn+ cards dealt 12+ end-of-turn damage. Died at 7 HP before Turn 16 Inferno.
key_moments: **Zero poison cards offered during the entire run.** Card rewards offered: Blade Dance, Leg Sweep, Backflip, Glass Knife, Acrobatics, Dash. Shop stock had no poison cards. No Noxious Fumes, Deadly Poison, Catalyst, Bouncing Flask, or Crippling Cloud appeared as options. This was RNG denial of the kill condition.

**Gremlin Nob cost 28 HP (70->42).** Deck had 9 Skills vs 5 Attacks. Despite knowing the Enrage mechanic, player was forced to play 7 Skills across 7 turns for survival (+14 Str to Nob). Piercing Wail spent on Turn 3 to reduce Nob's incoming -- this card was supposed to be saved for Hexaghost Inferno. Speed Potion + Leg Sweep used for emergency blocking on Turn 5.

**Piercing Wail never used against Hexaghost Inferno.** It was spent during the Nob fight. On the actual Inferno turn (Turn 9), player had only Strike + Strike + Defend -- took 11 damage. Piercing Wail would have reduced Inferno to 0.

**Smoke Bomb attempted on Turn 16** when player realized 7 HP vs incoming Inferno was lethal. Smoke Bomb cannot be used in boss fights -- the escape failed and the player played remaining cards knowing the fight was lost.

**Zero damage in 7 of 8 hallway fights.** Excellent combat execution -- Neutralize+ (innate from Ring of the Snake draw + Lantern 4E turn 1) + Backflip (innate) + Leg Sweep + Dash provided complete blocking. The deck was defensively excellent but had no boss kill condition.

**Hexaghost at ~68/250 HP (27%) when player died.** If poison had been available, the fight would have ended 5+ turns earlier. Confirmed: the sixth Silent Hexaghost death from insufficient damage output.
**Glass Knife damage degradation observed:** 16 -> 12 -> 8 damage across three uses within the same combat (8x2 -> 6x2 -> 4x2 base, with hits reducing by 2 each subsequent play).
lessons: **The Silent MUST have poison scaling or damage amplifiers to kill Hexaghost.** This is the 6th confirmed death from insufficient damage. Defensive excellence cannot compensate. If no poison or amplifiers are drafted by Floor 10 against Hexaghost, the run is likely doomed.
**Save Piercing Wail for the boss when Hexaghost is visible.** Spending it on Nob saved ~9 HP but cost 36+ HP from the unmitigated Inferno. Net: -27 HP trade.

**Gremlin Nob + Skill-heavy Silent deck = 25-30 HP drain.** The fight is nearly unwinnable without taking massive damage. Consider whether the elite relic is worth the HP cost when Hexaghost is the boss and no rest site follows.
**Smoke Bomb does not work in boss fights.** The player attempted to escape and it failed. Do not count on Smoke Bomb for boss fight survival.
**Glass Knife degrades within combat** -- each subsequent play deals 4 less total damage (2 less per hit). Strong first use (16 dmg for 1E) but diminishing returns over a long fight.
